field_help.tool_name: Name of the tool. Example: Clustal Omega
field_help.tool_url: URL to the official website or documentation of the tool. Example: http://www.clustal.org/omega/
field_help.tool_description: Short description of what the tool does. Example: a multiple sequence alignment of protein or nucleotide sequences
field_help.docker_command: Full Docker command to run the tool, including mounted volume and parameters. Example: docker run --rm -v /your/data/dir:/data pegi3s/clustalomega -i input.fasta -o output.fasta
field_help.directory_description: Describe what should be inside `/your/data/dir`. Example: the FASTA file you want to align.
field_help.input_file: Name of the input file used in the command. Example: input.fasta
field_help.input_description: Explain what the input file represents. Example: FASTA file containing sequences to align
field_help.output_file: Name of the output file generated by the tool. Example: output.fasta
field_help.output_description: Explain what the output file contains. Example: aligned FASTA file
field_help.tool_url_help: URL to the help/documentation page of the tool (can be the same as tool_url if no separate help page exists). Example: https://www.ebi.ac.uk/seqdb/confluence/display/THD/Clustal+Omega
field_help.help_command: Command to display tool help inside Docker. Example: docker run --rm pegi3s/clustalomega --help

placeholder_hints.output_description: `output_file` to the actual name of your ...

field_help.tool_name: Name of the tool. Example: cd-hit
field_help.original_image: Name of the original Docker image being reused. Example: biocontainers/cd-hit
field_help.source_dockerfile: Where the original Dockerfile can be found (e.g. DockerHub, GitHub). Example: DockerHub
field_help.dockerfile_url: Direct URL to the original Dockerfile or repository. Example: https://hub.docker.com/r/biocontainers/cd-hit
field_help.docker_command: Full Docker command to run the tool, including mounted volume and parameters. Example: docker run --rm -v /your/data/dir:/data pegi3s/cd-hit -i input.fasta -o output.fasta
field_help.directory_description: Describe what should be inside `/your/data/dir`. Example: the FASTA file you want to align.
field_help.input_file: Name of the input file used in the command. Example: input.fasta
field_help.input_description: Explain what the input file represents. Example: FASTA file containing sequences to align
field_help.output_file: Name of the output file generated by the tool. Example: output.fasta
field_help.output_description: Explain what the output file contains. Example: aligned FASTA file
field_help.tool_url_help: URL to the official documentation or help page of the tool. Example: https://www.bioinformatics.org/cd-hit/
field_help.help_command: Command to display tool help inside Docker. Example: docker run --rm pegi3s/cd-hit --help

field_help.tool_name: Name of the tool. Example: auto-pss-genome
field_help.manual_url: URL to the official documentation or user guide of the tool. Example: https://github.com/pegi3s/auto-pss-genome
field_help.source_url: URL to the source code repository (e.g. GitHub, GitLab). Example: https://github.com/pegi3s/auto-pss-genome
field_help.dockerhub_url: URL to the Docker Hub image page. Example: https://hub.docker.com/r/pegi3s/auto-pss-genome
